title: Shinjis Nationality
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's Nationality Post by Wonderboy » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:21 pm Well I've had this on my mind for a while now. I've been wondering what nationality Shinji comes from, automatically most people would say Japanese of course. I know this is nothing more then a stereotype, but I've recently noticed Shinji's eyes are a dark shade of blue. While to most that may mean nothing, but blue eyes on an Asian, whether it be Chinese, Japanese, or Korean, is fairly uncommon. Now this brings me to a speculation that maybe Shinji is a mixture of two races o.o, this is rather unlikely since both of his parents (I assume) are of Japanese descent, both having Japanese last names and what not (Ikari and Rokubungi) Maybe I'm thinking to much, but I'm just curious as to what Shinji's nationality is.

[]: Post by BattleMonkey » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:30 pm It's an anime....

[]: Post by IrkenEvangelion » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:36 pm If that was the answer to every question we would have these forums.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:45 pm Standard anime physics explain hair and eye colours. Those are a given of the form.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:46 pm BattleMonkey wrote: It's an anime....

[]: Post by schismatics » Mon Jul 20, 2009 2:49 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I haven't seen an anime yet that has characters that actually look Japanese in them. They all look very Caucasian.

[]: Post by oOoOoOo » Mon Jul 20, 2009 5:17 pm PRO-TIP: Anime characters do not look like any nationality because they are hand-drawn cartoons. To the Japanese, anime characters look Japanese. To Westerners, they look Western. And so on. PRO-TIP #2: Cultures, like people, are self-centred.

[]: Post by Barong » Mon Jul 20, 2009 5:19 pm But to me, they look weird mostly. What are you implying!!!11

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jul 20, 2009 5:32 pm ^who is THEY, anyway?

[]: Post by BattleMonkey » Mon Jul 20, 2009 5:39 pm Normal Japanese female:

[]: Post by NAveryW » Mon Jul 20, 2009 6:16 pm Yeah, I noticed Misato's hair is purple. I think that means she's probably an alien.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Jul 20, 2009 7:20 pm Rei's eyes are red Her hair is blue Misato can be a bitch So what are--- Oh wait.... ============ In all seriousness I don't think his eye color really means anything. I do think Shinji is Japanese and probably nothing else(Has anyone for a brief moment ever pictured Gendo being part Scottish?). I've recently noticed Shinji's eyes are a dark shade of blue.

[]: Post by Rock-Slash » Mon Jul 20, 2009 7:27 pm Shinji is Brazilian.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 20, 2009 9:54 pm It's just an anime. The medium itself allows the artist to express character traits through unnatural color pallets, and because it's all stylized character design anyway, having unnatural colors wouldn't be as distracting as it would if it were live-action. I have no idea when nationality Shinji looks like (I like the point oOoOoOo made), simply because the color pallet in his character design were only used to reveal his personality in some way, not nationality.

[]: Post by Reichu » Mon Jul 20, 2009 10:10 pm oOoOoOo wrote: Words

[]: Post by Timstuff » Tue Jul 21, 2009 12:15 am Anime characters (of the typical variety) are hyper idealized, so they don't really look like anyone.

[]: Post by Sammaeloo » Tue Jul 21, 2009 2:35 am Maybe there could be a race of people called Animesians.

[]: Post by TheLobe » Tue Jul 21, 2009 6:15 am when you actually compare them to Japanese people they look more realistic if you assume they're Japanese. Many animes have blonde's, as well as blues and purples. Mostly as they tend to be rather stylin', so it can be assume that it's dyed, as is possible with Misato, but it seems mighty unlikely in the case of Rei, and by now their is so many animes that its probably rarely though of as dyed, and more just character designs. and fyi misatos hair is blue not purple, why do people say that.

[]: Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Jul 21, 2009 9:51 am Sammaeloo wrote: Maybe there could be a race of people called Animesians.

[]: Post by Iron Rooster » Tue Jul 21, 2009 11:09 am oOoOoOo wrote: PRO-TIP: Anime characters do not look like any nationality because they are hand-drawn cartoons. To the Japanese, anime characters look Japanese. To Westerners, they look Western. And so on. PRO-TIP #2: Cultures, like people, are self-centred.
